summary:
The most common site for MNTI is the maxilla due to its high incidence at 62%, significantly exceeding other sites like the mandible or skull. Recurrence rates do not affect this finding since they pertain to treatment follow-up, not initial diagnosis location.

The most common site for MNTI is the maxilla, with a documented 62% prevalence, making it more frequent than other sites such as the mandible or skull. Initial presentation statistics support this conclusion; recurrence trends are irrelevant to determining the predominant anatomic origin.

Also notes that although mandibles may recur often after therapy, those details relate to outcome/post-treatment, not where tumors first appear—the core point being 'site' meaning onset/location/diagnosis.
